def: Not irritable; esp. (Physiol.), incapable of being stimulatedto action, as a muscle.-- In*ir`ri*ta*bil"i*ty, n.
The stem of an aftershaft or hypoptilum. [Written alsohyporachis.]
Expression of the passions; the science of the signs by whichhuman passions are indicated.

An officer who bore an ax and fasces or rods, as ensigns of hisoffice. His duty was to attend the chief magistrates when theyappeared in public, to clear the way, and cause due respect to bepaid to them, also to apprehend and punish criminals.Lictors and rods, the ensigns of their power. Milton.

An artificial channel into which water is let by a sluice;specifically, a trough constructed over the bed of a stream, so thatlogs, lumber, or rubbish can be floated down to some convenient placeof delivery.
Having a propensity to venery; lustful; lecherous. Dryden.-- Sa*la"cious*ly, dv.-- Sa*la"cious*ness, n.
The cloudberry (Rudus Chamæmorus); -- so called from itsknotted stems.
Neglect or failure to prepare; want of preparation.
The practice of boxing, or fighting with the fist.
